name: Confluent MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Confluent MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Confluent Cloud — managing Kafka topics, producers/consumers, Schema Registry, connectors, and Flink SQL for real-time event streaming and data pipelines.
use_cases: Publishing events to Kafka topics from data pipeline agents
Creating and managing Kafka topics and partitions from infrastructure agents
Querying Schema Registry for event schema discovery
Monitoring consumer lag and cluster health from observability agents
Managing Kafka Connect connectors from data integration agents
Running Flink SQL queries for stream processing agents
not_for: Teams using AWS MSK, Azure Event Hubs, or Redpanda
Simple message queues — use RabbitMQ or SQS instead
best_when: An agent needs to interact with Confluent Cloud Kafka for event streaming, real-time pipelines, or data integration — especially in event-driven architectures.
avoid_when: You're using AWS MSK, Azure Event Hubs, or a self-managed Kafka cluster without Confluent Cloud.

auth.notes: Confluent Cloud API keys with cloud-level and cluster-level scope. Service accounts recommended for agents. RBAC roles control resource access.

pricing.paid_tiers: Consumption-based: $0.10+/GB throughput; Flink adds compute cost
pricing.notes: Confluent Cloud is consumption-based. Free trial available. Self-hosted Confluent Platform is separate licensing. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: Admin API rate limits vary by tier; Kafka produce/consume throughput scales with cluster

known_agent_gotchas: Environment ID + Cluster ID required for most operations — discover these first
Two API key levels: cloud (management) and cluster (produce/consume) — use correct level
Kafka topics require partition count upfront — cannot reduce partitions later
Schema Registry has separate auth from Kafka cluster
Consumer groups require careful management — agents should clean up after use
Flink SQL is separate from Kafka — requires Flink environment setup
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. RBAC with granular permissions. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, PCI DSS, GDPR. Confluent enterprise security.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. RBAC. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, PCI DSS, GDPR. Enterprise Confluent security.
